code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test23-FEB-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function with possible tubular involvement. Low total CO₂ may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. Hemoglobin A1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria, possibly due to recent onset hyperglycemia or analytical interference. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and diabetes mellitus.
